description: The Disclosure and Barring Service helps employers make safer recruitment decisions. DBS is an executive non-departmental public body, sponsored by the Home Office .

details.body:
The Disclosure and Barring Service helps employers make safer recruitment decisions.

DBS is an executive non-departmental public body, sponsored by the Home Office.

ordered_featured_documents.summary: The Disclosure and Barring Service has published its 2026 to 2027 business plan, outlining priorities for improving services and delivering better public outcomes.
ordered_featured_documents.title: DBS launches its 2026-2027 business plan

ordered_featured_documents.summary: Disclosure and Barring Service (DBS) is expanding its Faith in Safeguarding campaign with free pop-up clinics across England.
ordered_featured_documents.title: DBS expands Faith in Safeguarding campaign nationally

ordered_featured_documents.summary: DBS has launched a podcast for employers and safeguarding professionals covering topics such as DBS checks, eligibility and the barring process.
ordered_featured_documents.title: DBS launches new podcast for employers and safeguarding professionals

ordered_featured_documents.summary: From 21 January 2026, self-employed people and personal employees can apply for Enhanced and Enhanced with Barred List(s) DBS checks through an Umbrella Body.
ordered_featured_documents.title: Self-employed workers and personal employees can now apply for Enhanced DBS checks

ordered_featured_documents.summary: The Disclosure and Barring Service (DBS) has launched a new Customer Satisfaction Survey, to collect feedback from Disclosure and Customer Service customers.
ordered_featured_documents.title: DBS launches new Customer Satisfaction Survey to improve services

ordered_featured_documents.summary: DBS has launched a new podcast video to help faith organisations understand their legal duty to refer safeguarding concerns and how to access support.
ordered_featured_documents.title: DBS publishes 'In Conversation With' video to support faith organisations with the legal duty to refer

ordered_featured_links.title: Apply for a basic DBS check
Check someone's criminal record as an employer
Track or view your DBS certificate
Update Service and other online services

details.secondary_corporate_information_pages: Read about the types of information we routinely publish in our Publication scheme. Find out about our commitment to publishing in Welsh. Our Personal information charter explains how we treat your personal information. Read our policy on Social media use.

details.body: The Member of the Armed Forces Pay Review Body is responsible for providing independent advice to the Prime Minister and the Secretary of State for Defence on the remuneration and charges for members of the Naval, Military and Air Forces of the Crown.

Some of the Chair’s responsibilities at the DBS include:

  • formulating the DBS board’s strategy, discharging its statutory duties, and leading the board in overseeing the timely production of realistic business and forward plans
  • monitoring in-year performance and examining change proposals with rigour to maintain focus on delivery
  • ensuring that the DBS board, in reaching decisions, takes proper account of guidance provided by the responsible Minister and sponsor department
  • ensuring that DBS board members adhere to the code of practice for members of Executive Non-Departmental Public Bodies

role_appointments.title: Dr Gillian Fairfield - DBS Board Chairman

details.body:
Board members are senior figures from outside DBS who bring a mix of expertise and skills from across the public and private sector. They all:

  • oversee performance
  • set the strategic direction for the DBS
  • support and challenge management on implementing the DBS strategic direction
  • make sure that statutory duties are carried out

DBS board members are appointed following open and fair competition and in line with the Governance Code on Public Appointments. The appointments are for an initial period of 3 years, with the possibility of re-appointment.

details.body:
Some of the Chief Executive’s responsibilities include:

  • working with the Chair, non-executive directors and executive directors to deliver DBS’ statutory safeguarding responsibilities
  • working with the DBS board to develop the strategic plan for the organisation.
  • promoting the DBS corporate strategy, vision and values to inspire and motivate all staff through an open and transparent culture
  • leading the Executive Leadership Team (ELT) and broader Senior Delivery Team (SDT) to ensure that DBS offers the highest quality of service to our customers
  • working with and advising the board on the operational implications of government policy and legislative developments concerning DBS
  • ensuring DBS uses modern digital technologies to improve productivity, efficiency and customer service

role_appointments.title: Dr Jeff James CBE - Chief Executive

details.body:
Some of the Deputy Chief Executive & Executive Director of Operations responsibilities include:

  • reporting directly to the Chief Executive Officer (CEO), accountable for all operational service delivery and performance in DBS
  • managing the operational and financial performance of key service delivery partners, and managing relationships and compliance of the Registered Body network
  • establishing and delivering the operating plan for the disclosure function in line with the DBS strategic and business plan
  • establishing and maintaining positive relationships with stakeholders

role.title: Deputy Chief Executive & Executive Director of Operations

role_appointments.title: Ian Johnston - Director for Operations (Disclosure) and DBS Board Member

details.body:
The Board Secretary is a pivotal role within the organisation.

The principal advisor to the Board and the Executive Team on all aspects of Corporate Governance.

The Board Secretary supports the Chair to ensure that DBS’ Board is compliant and operates effectively.

The Executive Director of Technology and Innovation leads the organisation’s major technical modernisation, using emerging technologies to enhance employee experience, boost efficiency, and support high-quality service delivery and in line with the developing target operating model.

Overseeing a £50 million, three-year technology refresh programme to enable future safeguarding priorities, value for money and significant benefits to our customers.

Key priorities include eliminating paper-based processes, adopting best-in-class SaaS solutions, and favouring off-the-shelf technologies.

The role champions Artificial Intelligence adoption in line with the government’s agenda, improving staff productivity and user engagement.

role.title: Acting Executive Director of Technology and Innovation

role_appointments.title: Steve Gardner - Interim Executive Director of Technology and Innovation

details.body:
The Executive Director of People and Inclusion leads the strategic direction and delivery of the people agenda at the Disclosure and Barring Service (DBS).

As a key member of the Executive Team, the role will include human resources, organisational development, equality, diversity & inclusion and staff wellbeing.

They champion continuous improvement, transformation, and employee engagement to ensure DBS remains an agile, high-performing, and inclusive public service employer.

role.title: Acting Executive Director of People and Inclusion

role_appointments.title: Joanne Kelland-Owen - Interim HR & Organisational Development Director

details.body:
The Executive Director of Strategy and Impact provides inspirational leadership of the development and delivery of the Disclosure and Barring Service (DBS) strategy, policy, performance management and insight functions.

With overall responsibility for business planning, partnerships and communications, the Executive Director works closely with the Chief Executive, the Executive and the Board to translate DBS’ strategic vision into operational delivery.

details.body:
The Executive Director of Finance and Commercial Services leads the Finance and Commercial functions, providing strategic guidance to support the organisation’s vision and objectives.

As a key member of the Executive Team they are responsible for both operational financial management and the development and delivery of commercial strategy, ensuring robust financial control, risk management, and the identification and realisation of new business opportunities.

They also provide strategic and financial guidance to the Accounting Oﬃcer.

role.title: Acting Executive Director of Finance and Commercial Services

role_appointments.title: Neil Curtis - Acting Executive Director of Finance and Commercial Services

details.description:
Our current opening hours are 09:00 to 17:00, Monday to Friday.

The Disclosure and Barring Service webchat is available Monday to Friday from 09:00 to 17:00. The webchat is available to answer general customer queries. 

If emailing us, please provide 5 pieces of information, this must include: 
•	Full name
•	Address declared on the application form (including house number and postcode) 
•	Date of birth

And two of the below: 

•	DBS Application reference number
•	National Insurance Number
•	Position applied for 
•	Employer Name
•	Place of Birth (town/city and country)
•	DBS certificate reference number
•	Passport Number
•	Driving Licence number
•	Previous address

In addition to the above, Registered Bodies will also need to provide their ‘Registered Body reference number’ and ‘Countersignatory reference number’ to confirm their identity.

You may be asked some security questions to verify your identity. All calls to the above number are recorded for record keeping, training and quality assurance.

We cannot guarantee the security of information until it is in our possession, and will not take responsibility for such information until we receive it.

Go to the DBS online tracking service to check on the progress of an application. Tracking an application is much quicker online. The link to this service is available at the top of this homepage.
details.title: DBS disclosure service

details.description:
Relay UK: 

Customers who cannot hear or speak on the phone can contact DBS using the number above, or by downloading the Relay UK app. When you make a phone call with your smartphone, tablet or computer using this service, a Relay Assistant will act as a go-between for your conversation.
 
You type what you want to say, and the Relay Assistant will relay your words to DBS. The Relay Assistant can also type the words DBS says to you, so that you can read it.
 
Convo: 

If you are a British Sign Language (BSL) user, you can also use Convo to connect to DBS via an Interpreter.
 
Just log in to the Convo app on iOS, Android, or a web browser and find the Disclosure and Barring Service in the Community Directory.
 
To find out how to download the Relay UK or Convo apps, please refer to our Accessibility Support guidance that is linked above ('Contact: DBS accessible contact options')

phone_numbers.title: Relay UK (if you cannot hear or speak on the phone):

details.title: DBS accessible contact options

details.description:
Contact us if you think your DBS certificate is wrong. This can include incorrect personal details, incorrect conviction information or other discrepancies. If you receive a DBS certificate with a conviction that doesn't relate to you, you should also inform the person who requested that you complete the form as soon as possible.

The phone line for certificate disputes is open 9am to 5pm, Monday to Friday only.
details.title: DBS certificate disputes

details.description:
Please note: Advisers who answer calls to the sensitive applications line cannot answer queries that do not relate to sensitive applications.

Transgender applicants are protected in legislation and are not obliged to include details indicating a previous gender in their application form.

DBS has a process in place which allows any details indicating a previous gender to be checked without being disclosed on the completed certificate.

They are, however, required by law to provide all name details to DBS in their current and any previous identity. Deliberately withholding this information may be an attempt to prevent conviction information being revealed and is an offence.

If emailing, we cannot guarantee the security of information until it is in our possession, and will not take responsibility for such information until we receive it.

details.title: Sensitive applications

details.description: We cannot guarantee the security of information until it is in our possession, and will not take responsibility for such information until we receive it. For this reason we recommend submitting referrals via our secure online referral service. However, if you are using the mailing address, we recommend using registered post.
details.title: DBS barring

details.description:
If you wish to submit a new request under the Freedom of Information Act please contact the DBS.

Most requests are free of charge but you may need to pay a small amount for photocopies or postage. When you submit a request we'll let you know if you have to make a payment.
details.title: FOI requests

details.description: Addresses for service of legal documents. Service is accepted by post or by email.
details.title: DBS legal services

details.description:
If you are unhappy with a DBS service, please contact DBS complaints. To help us investigate your complaint, please complete the form linked above.

You can only make enquiries or complaints about your own DBS check. Only Registered Bodies can make enquiries or complaints about another person's DBS check. Employers and other third parties should ask the applicant to contact us directly.
details.title: DBS complaints

details.description:
Customers who have used Disclosure services to obtain a DBS certificate or have contacted DBS Customer Services for support can provide feedback on their experience by completing this approx. 5-minute survey. The survey is available in English and Welsh.

Please note, this survey should not be used to submit a complaint about DBS services. If you would like to submit a complaint, please do so using the contact details in the 'complaints' section above.
details.title: Customer Satisfaction Survey

details.description:
Residents of Guernsey, Jersey and the Isle of Man may be entitled to refunds for payments made for certain Disclosure and Barring Service (DBS) products.

For more information, please refer to the guidance provided in the link above. If you believe you meet the eligibility criteria for a refund, kindly complete the form included within the guidance document.
details.title: Crown Dependencies Refunds

ordered_roles.title: Chief Executive and DBS Board Member

details.body:
Some of the Chief Executive’s responsibilities include:

  • overall management and staffing of the organisation and leading a team of directors
  • developing the DBS strategic plan and delivering its objectives and policy intent
  • securing and protecting the vision of the strategic plan
  • ensuring that DBS is governed responsibly, reported honestly and escalated appropriately
  • being the accounting officer with responsibility for ensuring that DBS remains self-funded and adheres to the principles of ‘managing public money’
  • engaging effectively with stakeholders
  • Senior Responsible Owner (SRO) for the R1 project, which is the DBS modernisation programme

ordered_roles.title: Chief Executive and DBS Board Member

  • establishing and maintaining positive relationships with stakeholders

details.body:
Some of the Director for Operations (Barring) responsibilities include:

  • reporting directly to the Chief Operating Officer (COO), accountable for all barring operational service delivery and performance in DBS
  • leading the barring function ensuring delivery of safeguarding, ensuring that processes are robust and comply with public sector governance arrangements
  • establishing and delivering the operating plan for the barring function in line with the DBS strategic and business plan
  • establishing and maintaining positive relationships with stakeholders

Some of the Deputy Chief Executive and Executive Director of Finance and Corporate Services responsibilities include:

  • reporting directly to the Chief Executive Officer (CEO) and having strategic oversight of finance, commercial and legal
  • providing expert guidance to the DBS board and its committees in relation to specialist areas
  • being the lead contact for ensuring effective relationships with the wider Home Office, and leading on specific areas of work relating to ministerial priorities for the organisation in conjunction with the CEO
  • being the lead officer for interpreting financial, policy, legal and commercial guidelines applicable to DBS, providing advice to the board including commissioning audits to ensure compliance with standards
  • being the lead officer for developing procurement, commercial strategies and contract negotiations at senior levels for DBS, aligned to commercial best practice
  • appointed SIRO for DBS with delegated responsibilities and accountability for information risk management

Some of the Chief People Officer responsibilities include:

  • reporting directly to the Chief Executive Officer (CEO), delivering leadership resilience and developing people practices, including talent management, welfare and culture
  • implementing a strategy to ensure succession planning by conducting an annual engagement survey and monitoring quarterly
  • developing and delivering the people strategy which includes the elements of workforce resourcing, employee relations, learning and facilities

The Executive Director of Finance and Commercial Services leads the Finance and Commercial functions, providing strategic guidance to support the organisation’s vision and objectives.

details.body:
Some of the Chief Information and Digital Officer’s responsibilities include:

  • ensuring the organisation manages, exploits and safeguards information to the best effect, implementing best practice in relation to information governance and managing risk
  • providing strategic and tactical planning for IT security and Identifying and mitigating IT and data related risks
  • defining the strategic direction and business priorities for the development of technology solutions, both IT and digital, and leading the development of an IT strategy
  • providing strong leadership and senior-level advocacy for the delivery of digitally driven solutions and to foster innovation
  • acting as a link between external IT providers and the business, working with the finance and resources directorate to procure services and manage supplier performance to ensure business needs are met
  • fully engaging and participating in the corporate change programme, acting as senior responsible owner for product and service delivery as required
  • acting as Senior Responsible Owner (SRO) on corporate projects ensuring the project of change meets its objectives, projected benefits and is conducted cost effectively

Some of the Director of Safeguarding’s responsibilities include:

  • reporting directly to the Chief Executive Officer (CEO), accountable for quality management, external relations, stakeholder engagement, research, customer services, strategy and policy and marketing

  • developing and championing a vision for safeguarding, strategy and quality across DBS

ordered_roles.title: Executive Director of Barring and Safeguarding

details.body:
Some of the Chief Operating Officer’s responsibilities include:

  • reporting directly to the Chief Executive Officer (CEO), accountable for all Disclosure and Barring operational activity
  • responsibility for the operational service delivery and performance across the Disclosure and Barring Service, which involves key activities around processing disclosure applications and evaluating barring cases
  • responsibility for the operational performance of the key service delivery partners (Local Police Forces and external BPO providers) and compliance management
  • a sponsorship role for corporate initiatives and business change projects and, where applicable, acting as Senior Responsible Owner (SRO)
  • implementing internal and external audit and legal review findings and policy changes in relation to Barring and Disclosure, implementing best practice and maintaining robust management controls and operational processes to support public safeguarding
  • acting as the lead Board member in representing Disclosure and Barring applications and cases, including working with the Director of Safeguarding to provide recommendations to the Board and its committees on complex Disclosure and Barring decisions
  • ensuring that Disclosure and Barring information is processed appropriately and that security controls are implemented within operations in the handling of third party data, working with corporate security teams to develop and implement secure and effective operational processes
  • contributing to the strategic direction of the DBS, providing recommendations, thought leadership and engaging with internal and external stakeholders

Some of the Chief People Officer’s responsibilities include:

  • being a Board member and reporting to the Chief Executive Officer (CEO)
  • responsibility for the People Directorate and delivery of all HR and Facilities services, policies and processes, as well as organisational capability strategy
  • the development and delivery of the People Strategy - setting organisational direction and creating clear deliverables
  • contributing to the strategic direction of the DBS, providing recommendations, thought leadership and engaging with internal and external stakeholders
  • working with other ALB/Civil Service/Home Office HR teams to influence and develop wider strategic thinking
  • developing and promoting a culture of continuous improvement and introducing a culture of social and corporate responsibility
  • developing new ways of work through changes in culture and smarter ways of working

Some of the Chief Executive’s responsibilities include:

  • working with the Chair, non-executive directors and executive directors to deliver DBS’ statutory safeguarding responsibilities
  • working with the DBS board to develop the strategic plan for the organisation.
  • promoting the DBS corporate strategy, vision and values to inspire and motivate all staff through an open and transparent culture
  • leading the Executive Leadership Team (ELT) and broader Senior Delivery Team (SDT) to ensure that DBS offers the highest quality of service to our customers
  • working with and advising the board on the operational implications of government policy and legislative developments concerning DBS
  • ensuring DBS uses modern digital technologies to improve productivity, efficiency and customer service

Disclosure and Barring Service

Some of the Director of Barring Operation’s responsibilities include:

  • delivering outstanding customer service and operational excellence against DBS performance targets and published service standards for Barring Operations
  • ensuring that resource, skills, and productivity levels can meet demand
  • ensuring barring processes are continually evaluated to ensure they reflect the wider safeguarding environment, and that lessons are learned from appeals and reviews
  • providing strategic and operational planning for Barring Operations, defining vision and values to inspire and motivate all staff through an open and transparent culture, demonstrating the OneDBS ethos, and supporting the DBS 2025 strategy
  • providing strong leadership and senior-level advocacy for the Barring delivery and fostering continuous improvement
  • building and promoting effective stakeholder and partner engagement and collaboration at strategic and operational levels
  • developing, fostering, and maintaining strong and effective relationships with all stakeholders across law enforcement, national and local government departments, the private sector, and voluntary sector

Board Apprentices join for one year to gain first-hand experience, through observation, of the workings and dynamics of Boards. They are identified through partnerships with the ‘Black on Board’ and ‘Boardroom Apprentice’ schemes. The role of Board Apprentice provides training and support, as well as an opportunity to gain experience, before taking the step to become a fully signed-up Board Member.

Board members are senior figures from outside DBS who bring a mix of expertise and skills from across the public and private sector. They all:

  • oversee performance
  • set the strategic direction for the DBS
  • support and challenge management on implementing the DBS strategic direction
  • make sure that statutory duties are carried out

DBS board members are appointed following open and fair competition and in line with the Governance Code on Public Appointments. The appointments are for an initial period of 3 years, with the possibility of re-appointment.

Board Apprentices join for one year to gain first-hand experience, through observation, of the workings and dynamics of Boards. They are identified through partnerships with the ‘Black on Board’ and ‘Boardroom Apprentice’ schemes. The role of Board Apprentice provides training and support, as well as an opportunity to gain experience, before taking the step to become a fully signed-up Board Member.

The Executive Director of Technology and Innovation leads the organisation’s major technical modernisation, using emerging technologies to enhance employee experience, boost efficiency, and support high-quality service delivery and in line with the developing target operating model.

Overseeing a £50 million, three-year technology refresh programme to enable future safeguarding priorities, value for money and significant benefits to our customers.

Key priorities include eliminating paper-based processes, adopting best-in-class SaaS solutions, and favouring off-the-shelf technologies.

The role champions Artificial Intelligence adoption in line with the government’s agenda, improving staff productivity and user engagement.

ordered_roles.title: Acting Executive Director of Technology and Innovation

details.body:
The Executive Director of People and Inclusion leads the strategic direction and delivery of the people agenda at the Disclosure and Barring Service (DBS).

As a key member of the Executive Team, the role will include human resources, organisational development, equality, diversity & inclusion and staff wellbeing.

They champion continuous improvement, transformation, and employee engagement to ensure DBS remains an agile, high-performing, and inclusive public service employer.

ordered_roles.title: Acting Executive Director of People and Inclusion

details.body:
The Executive Director of Strategy and Impact provides inspirational leadership of the development and delivery of the Disclosure and Barring Service (DBS) strategy, policy, performance management and insight functions.

With overall responsibility for business planning, partnerships and communications, the Executive Director works closely with the Chief Executive, the Executive and the Board to translate DBS’ strategic vision into operational delivery.

ordered_roles.title: Executive Director of Strategy and Impact

details.body:
The Executive Director of Finance and Commercial Services leads the Finance and Commercial functions, providing strategic guidance to support the organisation’s vision and objectives.

As a key member of the Executive Team they are responsible for both operational financial management and the development and delivery of commercial strategy, ensuring robust financial control, risk management, and the identification and realisation of new business opportunities.

They also provide strategic and financial guidance to the Accounting Oﬃcer.

ordered_roles.title: Acting Executive Director of Finance and Commercial Services

title: Disclosure and Barring Service
